source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Eugene Island Block 360

event.top_event: Struck by handrail during removal operation
actions_taken: Injured personnel transported by helicopter to medical facility
event.summary: On 12 February 2021 at approximately 0900 hours, a Quality Construction and Production crew was removing a heater tube from heat exchanger HBG-700 on Cox Operating's EI 360-E facility. During removal of a handrail, the third leg became stuck in the socket. The crew used a crane with a nylon strap to apply tension while attempting manual removal with hands and hammer. An employee positioned a 5-foot pry bar under the kick plate and stood up using his foot for leverage while another employee pushed the handrail. The handrail broke free under crane tension and struck the standing employee in the face, causing lacerations, fractured nose, and fractured orbital socket. The employee was transported offshore and admitted to hospital for orbital socket surgery on 16 February 2021.
key_phrases: Human Performance Error - Not following proper procedure
Improper use of tools and equipment

hazards.name: Mechanical energy from crane-assisted load removal
Struck-by hazard from suspended or tensioned objects

threats.name: Handrail breaks free under crane tension while personnel in strike zone
Improper job planning and hazard assessment for modified task
Crane operator applies tension without proper control or authority to stop

consequences.name: Personnel struck in face by handrail
Serious injury - lacerations, fractured nose, fractured orbital socket
Requirement for surgical intervention and hospitalization

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent hazard exposure

supporting_text: The AI found that stop work should have been implemented and the JSA should have been revised as new potential hazards were introduced due to the handrail's third leg being stuck in the socket.

Human Performance Error- Not following proper procedure: The construction crew attempted to dislodge the handrail by hand while tension was applied by the crane. As per the Job Safety Analysis (JSA), stop work should have been implemented and the JSA revised as the potential hazards changed from the original JSA.

controls.name: Stop Work Authority and Implementation
controls.barrier_role: Halt operations when hazards change or safety is uncertain
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent continuation of unsafe work

supporting_text: BSEE found that the probable cause of the injury was a failure to follow the JSA and implementing stop work.
The AI found that stop work should have been implemented and the JSA should have been revised as new potential hazards were introduced

controls.name: Cox Safety Environmental Management System Crane Operations Policy
controls.barrier_role: Prevent shock loading and unsafe crane operations
controls.lod_basis: Company policy to prevent shock loading hazards

supporting_text: the crane shall not be used in a manner that might result in shock loading (for example, pulling up grating that is still welded to the platform)
BSEE found that the contributing cause of the injury was failure to follow Cox's Safety Environmental Management System Crane Operations Policy General Operating Practices l

controls.name: API RP 2D 3.1.5 Crane Operator Authority
controls.barrier_role: Empower crane operator to refuse unsafe operations
controls.lod_basis: Industry standard requiring operator authority to stop operations

supporting_text: The Qualified Crane Operator (herein also called Crane Operator) is responsible for those operations under his or her direct control. Whenever there is any doubt as to safety, the Crane Operator should have the authority to stop and refuse to handle loads or continue operations as safety dictates.

BSEE found that the contributing cause of the injury was failure to follow Cox's Safety Environmental Management System Crane Operations Policy General Operating Practices l, and API RD 2D 3.1.5 Operating Practices.

controls.name: Personnel positioning and exclusion from strike zone
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from being in strike zone during load movement
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent struck-by injuries

supporting_text: The IP, repositioned himself, stood up and used his foot to gain more leverage while another QCP crew employee pushed the handrail with his hands. The handrail broke free under tension from the crane and struck the standing IP in the face.
Improper use of tools or equipment, regarding use of a prybar and crane in an unsafe manner.

controls.name: First Aid and Emergency Response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care to minimize injury severity
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to mitigate consequence severity

supporting_text: The IP was attended by the Offshore First Aider and transported onshore for medical evaluation.
First Aid was administered on the facility. The injured employee was transported by helicopter to a medical facility for additional treatment.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
